generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Converts electrical current (I_M) into torque (τ_M) as the primary actuator for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot. Operates within the restricted working range of [-90°, 90°] with maximum angular velocity of |q̇_max| = 75°/s."
]

instance GearTransmission : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Transforms rotational energy (torque τ_G) with a reduced gear ratio between the permanent magnet synchronous motor and cycloidal gearbox. Part of the first axis mechanical transmission system."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Further reduces torque (τ_out) for final output while maintaining precision in the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot. Incorporates cycloidal drive mechanics for high torque density."
]

instance Sinumerik840DController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller executing motion commands (position, speed, acceleration) and interfacing with the motor via torque/current control. Dynamically adjusts inputs based on simulated friction models."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision encoder mounted directly on the motor shaft measuring angular position (φ_M) and velocity (ω_M) for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderData
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Encoder positioned behind the gearbox measuring output angle (φ_out) to enable backlash and elasticity analysis by comparing with motor encoder readings."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderData
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring input current (I_M) to the motor for torque estimation using manufacturer-provided motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance SecondaryEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance MQTTStreamedData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time streaming of time-series data (I_M, φ_M, φ_out, etc.) from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller to Digital Shadow via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Sinumerik840DController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) describing the structural composition of the first axis components (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox) and their interconnections."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) for component-level modeling of motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox enabling independent simulation and analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable MATLAB Simulink model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction characteristics for real-time friction estimation in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkProcessingEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable MATLAB Simulink model implementing Stribeck friction characteristics accounting for stiction and breakaway effects in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkProcessingEnabler
]

instance MotorSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulates motor output (τ_M, φ_M) using input current (I_M) and integrated friction models for friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkProcessingEnabler
]

instance GearwheelSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulates gearwheel output (τ_G, φ_G) based on motor output and gear ratio incorporating friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkProcessingEnabler
]

instance CycloidalGearboxSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulates final output (τ_out, φ_out) incorporating cycloidal gearbox dynamics and friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkProcessingEnabler
]

instance MeasuredMotorOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real measured data trace (r_M) from motor encoder representing actual rotational output including friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance MeasuredGearwheelOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real measured data trace (r_G) from secondary encoder representing gearwheel output including friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance MeasuredFinalOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real measured data trace (r_out) from secondary encoder representing final system output including friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance SimulatedMotorOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Friction-compensated simulated data trace (δr_M) generated by processing components representing ideal motor output without friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTStreamedData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance SimulatedGearwheelOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Friction-compensated simulated data trace (δr_G) generated by processing components representing ideal gearwheel output without friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTStreamedData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance SimulatedFinalOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Friction-compensated simulated data trace (δr_out) generated by processing components representing ideal final output without friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTStreamedData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance InputCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured input current (I_M) data used for torque estimation and friction model calibration."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TorqueCalculationEnabler
]

instance TorqueDerivedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived torque values (τ_M, τ_G, τ_out) computed from measured current and encoder data using manufacturer-provided dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InputCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML (Systems Modeling Language) platform enabling structural (BDDs) and behavioral (IBDs) modeling of the robot's first axis components."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComponentLevelModelingService, StructuralModelingService
]

instance SimulinkProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink platform implementing executable friction models and simulating system behavior for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelingService, MultiPhysicsSimulationService
]

instance MQTTDataIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol enabling real-time streaming of sensor data (I_M, φ_out) from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller to Digital Shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataStreamingService
]

instance DigitalShadowReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM) providing structured framework for data traceability, source identification, and model integration."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTraceabilityService, ModelIntegrationService
]

instance TorqueCalculationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing framework for deriving torque values from current measurements using manufacturer-provided motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:enables TorqueDerivationService
]

instance FrictionAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing framework combining white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models for dynamic friction estimation."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, ModelRefinementService
]

// C6: Services
instance ComponentLevelModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Component-level SysML modeling service enabling independent simulation and analysis of motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox sub-systems."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentLevelInsight
]

instance StructuralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Structural modeling service using SysML BDDs to describe the composition and interconnections of first axis components."
    DTDFVocab:provides StructuralCompositionInsight
]

instance FrictionModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing executable friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) in Simulink for real-time friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionModelParameters
]

instance MultiPhysicsSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Multi-physics-aware simulation service comparing frictionless and friction-influenced behavior using Simulink models."
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationValidationInsight
]

instance RealTimeDataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling real-time streaming of time-series data from physical components to Digital Shadow via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeDataTraces
]

instance DataTraceabilityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring traceability of data points to their sources, models, and processing components via DSRM framework."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataSourceMetadata
]

instance ModelIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service integrating engineering models (SysML, Simulink) with real-time data for comprehensive system analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides IntegratedModelOutput
]

instance TorqueDerivationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service deriving torque values from current measurements using manufacturer-provided dynamics models and motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:provides TorqueDerivedData
]

instance FrictionIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service combining white-box and black-box models to dynamically estimate static, kinetic, and viscous friction in real-time."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionIdentificationResults
]

instance ModelRefinementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service dynamically updating friction models during operation to account for wear, temperature, or load changes."
    DTDFVocab:provides RefinedFrictionModels
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ComponentLevelInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into independent behavior and characteristics of motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox components derived from component-level modeling."
]

instance StructuralCompositionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural composition and interconnection details of first axis components as described by SysML BDDs."
]

instance FrictionModelParameters : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Parameters for Coulomb-Viscous and Stribeck friction models used for real-time friction compensation calculations."
]

instance FrictionCompensationData : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data representing friction-compensated system behavior used for control optimization and validation."
]

instance SimulationValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results comparing simulated frictionless behavior with real-world measurements to verify model assumptions."
]

instance RealTimeDataTraces : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time data traces (I_M, φ_M, φ_out) streamed from physical components to Digital Shadow for continuous analysis."
]

instance DataSourceMetadata : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Metadata linking data points to their physical sources, processing components, and model origins via DSRM framework."
]

instance IntegratedModelOutput : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Output from integrated engineering models combining SysML structural descriptions with Simulink dynamic simulations."
]

instance TorqueDerivedData : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Derived torque values (τ_M, τ_G, τ_out) computed from current measurements and encoder data for friction analysis."
]

instance FrictionIdentificationResults : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification results for static, kinetic, and viscous friction components quantified via deviations between measured and simulated data traces."
]

instance RefinedFrictionModels : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dynamically updated friction models accounting for operational wear, temperature, and load conditions for improved accuracy."
]

instance StaticFrictionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time detection and quantification of static friction components in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance KineticFrictionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time detection and quantification of kinetic friction components in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ViscousFrictionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time detection and quantification of viscous friction components in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ModelRefinementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic updates to friction models based on operational data to improve accuracy over time."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Parameter tuning for Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller based on friction-induced inaccuracies to achieve up to 30% reduction in motion errors."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenancePlanningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Historical data analysis identifying wear patterns or temperature-dependent friction trends for predictive maintenance planning."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ProcessValidationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Verification of simulation models against real-world behavior to validate assumptions (e.g., gearbox elasticity)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C12: Environment
instance FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Environment representing the first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot comprising mechanical and electrical components within working range [-90°, 90°]."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearTransmission, CycloidalGearbox, Sinumerik840DController, MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder, MotorCurrentSensor
]

// C13: Component
instance FrictionCompensatedParameters : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component containing friction-compensated parameters dynamically generated by Digital Shadow and provided to Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller for motion control."
    base:isContainedIn FirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance UnifiedDashboard : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component providing unified visualization of measured vs. simulated data traces (torque, angular velocity) for friction analysis and validation."
    base:isContainedIn FirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital shadow operates on a **real-time, continuous time scale** with synchronization aligned to the robot’s operational frequency. Key time-related aspects include:
  - **High-frequency data acquisition**: Sensors (encoders, current sensors) stream data at rates sufficient for real-time friction analysis (exact frequency not specified but implied by control loop requirements, e.g., 1 kHz or higher for precise motion control).
  - **Dynamic model updates**: Friction models are recalibrated during operation to adapt to changes (e.g., wear, temperature drift) with a **latency-aware processing pipeline** ensuring near-instantaneous feedback for control adjustments.
  - **Event-driven processing**: Data traces are processed in real-time to detect friction-induced deviations (e.g., torque discrepancies) and update simulations accordingly. The DS ensures **temporal alignment** between physical measurements and virtual representations for accurate comparison."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is organized as follows:
  - **Centralized DS Pipeline**: A single digital shadow instance processes data for the **first axis** of the MABI MAX 100 robot, integrating:
    - **Physical Sources**: Sensors (motor encoder, secondary encoder), controller (SINUMERIK 840D sl.), and live measurements (I_M, ω_out).
    - **Virtual Components**: Simulation models (SimModel_M, SimModel_GW, SimModel_GB), data calculation models (torque/velocity derivations), and processing components (e.g., Simulation_M) that generate ˆr_M, ˆr_G, ˆr_out.
    - **Data Flow**: Measured data → Processing (friction models) → Simulated outputs → Comparison with reference values → Dashboard visualization.
  - **Orchestration**:
    - **Purpose-driven**: The DS is designed to **understand friction relations** and **detect deviations** in the first axis, with a scope limited to **deviation analysis between simulated and measured data**.
    - **Model-Driven**: Uses **SysML** for structural modeling and **Simulink** for behavioral simulation, ensuring consistency between physical and virtual representations.
  - **Interoperability**: The DS integrates with the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller** via MQTT for real-time data exchange and implicit feedback for control adjustments."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital shadow operates primarily during the **operational phase** of the robot’s life cycle, focusing on **as-operated** representation (not as-designed or as-manufactured). Key stages include:
  - **Operational monitoring**: Real-time friction analysis and model refinement during robot operation (e.g., machining tasks).
  - **Continuous improvement**: Dynamic adaptation of friction models to account for wear, environmental changes, or tooling variations over time.
  - **Post-processing analysis**: Insights derived from historical data traces (e.g., friction trends) for maintenance planning or control optimization, though this is not explicitly detailed in the document."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The fidelity and validity of the digital twin are ensured through:
  - **Model Validation**:
    - **Simulink simulations** (Fig. 4a, 4b) validate friction models against ideal behavior (without friction).
    - **Comparison of measured vs. simulated data traces** (e.g., τ_M, ω_M) in the dashboard to detect discrepancies caused by friction.
  - **Uncertainty Handling**:
    - **Encoder differences**: The secondary encoder (behind the gearbox) reduces backlash and positional uncertainty by cross-referencing with the motor encoder.
    - **Parameter sensitivity**: Friction models account for variables like load, temperature, and time-dependent wear, though explicit uncertainty quantification is not detailed.
  - **Verification Mechanisms**:
    - **Traceability**: Each **DataPoint** in the DS is linked to its **Source** (sensor, simulation) and **Processing Component** (e.g., Simulation_M), enabling auditable validation.
    - **Dynamic model exchange**: The DS supports **real-time friction model updates** to adapt to operational changes, improving long-term validity.
  - **Error Sources**:
    - **Sensor noise**: Implicit in encoder readings (not quantified).
    - **Model approximation**: Analytical friction models may not capture all physical effects (e.g., Stribeck effect nuances), requiring data-driven refinement."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital shadow (DS) includes:
  - **Network Protocol**: **MQTT** for real-time streaming of sensor data (e.g., I_M, ω_out) from the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller** to the DS.
  - **Data Format**: Time-series data traces (e.g., arrays of torque τ, angular velocity ω) with metadata (e.g., time stamps, source identifiers).
  - **Latency**: Implicitly low-latency to support real-time friction analysis and control adjustments (exact latency not specified but critical for motion accuracy).
  - **Architecture**:
    - **Client-Server**: The DS acts as a **passive receiver** of data from the PT (digital shadow characteristic), while active processing (e.g., friction model updates) occurs in the DS pipeline.
    - **Decoupled Communication**: The DS does not push data back to the PT (unlike a digital twin), but processed insights (e.g., friction parameters) could theoretically be fed back to the controller for closed-loop control (not implemented here)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital shadow is hosted and deployed as follows:
  - **Execution Environment**: **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink** for simulation models and **custom processing components** (e.g., friction model calculators) running on a **local or cloud-based server** (not explicitly specified).
  - **Dashboard**: Visualization is implemented via a **unified dashboard** (technology not detailed) that displays:
    - Measured data traces (I_M, r_M, r_G, r_out).
    - Simulated data traces (ˆr_M, ˆr_G, ˆr_out).
    - Comparative analysis (e.g., torque deviations due to friction).
  - **Scalability**: The DS architecture is modular, allowing for **deployment on edge devices** (e.g., robot controller) or centralized servers, though the current implementation is not detailed beyond the first axis."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to the following standards and frameworks:
  - **Modeling Standards**:
    - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**: Used for structural (BDDs) and behavioral (IBDs) modeling of the robot’s first axis (ISO/IEC 19505).
    - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Implicitly referenced for semantic interoperability (IEC 62362, Industry 4.0 standards).
  - **Simulation Standards**:
    - **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink**: Industry-standard tool for dynamic system simulation.
  - **Data Exchange**:
    - **MQTT (OASIS Standard)**: Protocol for real-time data streaming between the PT and DS.
  - **Digital Shadow Framework**:
    - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: Customized for this use case based on [15] and [38], though not formally standardized beyond the referenced papers."
]
